mechanism: Inhibits the anti-apoptotic protein BCL-2 to promote apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for CLL/AML combos; notes mention synergy with hypomethylating agents; range given as 0.1 nM–10 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits anti-apoptotic BCL-2 family proteins (BCL-2 and BCL-XL) to promote apoptosis
additional_notes: Also referred to as ABT-263; range given as 1 nM–10 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits Src family kinases and ABL to block oncogenic signaling
additional_notes: Range given as 0.5 nM–10 µM; described as a CML/ALL drug.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including BCR-ABL; FLT3 activity reported
additional_notes: Range given as 0.5 nM–5 µM; notes mention solubility/cytotoxicity can be limiting.

mechanism: Multi-target kinase inhibition (including FLT3 contextually) to suppress proliferation signaling
additional_notes: Mentioned as used off-label in FLT3-ITD AML contexts; range given as 1 nM–20 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple receptor tyrosine kinases to reduce growth/survival signaling
additional_notes: Range given as 1 nM–20 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases involved in tumor growth and angiogenesis signaling
additional_notes: Range given as 1 nM–20 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits ALK, ROS1, and MET kinase signaling
additional_notes: Also reported to hit other kinases; range given as 1 nM–20 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits EGFR tyrosine kinase signaling
additional_notes: Notes: multiple reports of AML cell effects at µM range; solubility warning—consider 20–30 µM if precipitation occurs; range given as 10 nM–50 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits EGFR tyrosine kinase signaling
additional_notes: Range given as 10 nM–50 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 in the MAPK pathway to block downstream ERK signaling
additional_notes: Range given as 0.1 nM–1 µM; note suggests keeping top low due to potency.

mechanism: Inhibits MEK to suppress MAPK/ERK signaling
additional_notes: Range given as 0.1 nM–2 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits mTOR signaling to reduce protein synthesis and cell growth
additional_notes: Range given as 0.1 nM–10 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits mTOR signaling to reduce growth and proliferation
additional_notes: Range given as 0.1 nM–10 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits CDK4/6 to block G1→S cell-cycle progression
additional_notes: Range given as 1 nM–20 µM; note says many AML lines aren’t classic CDK4/6-dependent but it’s often tested.

mechanism: Inhibits CDK4/6 to arrest the cell cycle in G1
additional_notes: Range given as 1 nM–20 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits CDK4/6 (with broader CDK activity noted) to block cell-cycle progression
additional_notes: Described as having broader CDK activity; range given as 1 nM–20 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits the proteasome, disrupting protein degradation and inducing stress/apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for myeloma; range given as 0.1 nM–1 µM; note: often very potent—avoid unnecessarily high doses.

mechanism: Inhibits the proteasome to impair protein turnover and promote apoptosis
additional_notes: Range given as 0.1 nM–1 µM.

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and impairs autophagosome-lysosome function to inhibit autophagy
additional_notes: Two related drugs listed together (chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine); note: high in-vitro concentrations often needed for autophagy blockade; monitor general toxicity.
